question: A company tracks average website sign-ups per year. According to the chart, which statement about the most recent year is appropriate?

options.A: The most recent year has clearly the lowest sign-ups of the entire period.
options.B: The most recent year shows that sign-ups will keep decreasing in future years.
options.C: From this partial-year data, none of the conclusions about the full-year trend are justified.
options.D: The most recent year already shows a sharp drop compared with previous full years.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 4 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (363 sign-ups) directly to full-year values like 1,043 sign-ups creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
